title: ugni myricoides
intro: Ugni myricoides, also known as Chilean guava or uñín, is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. Native to the southern Andes, this shrub has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ugni myricoides include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, as well as its use as an antiseptic and wound healer. Herbal preparations of Ugni myricoides may be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used in traditional medicine in Chile and other parts of South America.
